{"society_power": 6.0, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 1.5, "state_change": -0.2, "key_event": "Gandhi's mass Non-Cooperation Movement transforms Congress into mass politics", "reasoning": "Society leapt from elite petitioning to all-India mass mobilization (satyagraha, Non-Cooperation, Khilafat, expanding press and a nationalist bourgeoisie), reaching moderate-to-strong associational capacity. The state remained formidably strong administratively and militarily, but Jallianwala Bagh and mass defiance dented its legitimacy and it made real (if controlled) concessions via the 1919 reforms, so its effective coercive dominance slipped slightly."}
